tool_args.label: New York City Transit Key Performance Metrics - July 2023

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.publisher: New York City Transit & Bus Committee
payload.description: Key performance metrics report prepared for the July 2023 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee. Covers customer satisfaction, ridership, service performance, accessibility, financial results, customers and communities, and safety and security.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Operates all subways and buses in New York City alongside MTA Bus. Employs 45,000 employees serving 4,500,000 passengers a day.
North America's largest transportation network, serving 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area. Governed by a 23-member Board of Directors.
Operates all subways and buses in New York City alongside New York City Transit.

tool_args.label: New York City Transit & Bus Committee

payload.entity_name: New York City Transit & Bus Committee

payload.description: MTA paratransit service.
MTA Police focusing efforts at Penn Station, Grand Central Terminal, Atlantic Av-Barclays Ctr, and Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av.

tool_args.label: Increased weekend service on G, J, M lines announced

payload.event_name: Increased weekend service on G, J and M lines
payload.description: President Richard Davey, Senior Vice President of Subways Demetrius Crichlow, and local officials announced at Hoyt-Schermerhorn Sts Station there will be increased weekend service on the G, J and M lines. A second batch of R211 subway cars also went into service that day.

tool_args.label: First R211 subway train in-service test completed

payload.event_name: First R211 subway train in-service test completed
payload.description: The first R211 subway train successfully completed its in-service test, and the second train is now in service. Deliveries will now be up to two trains per month.

tool_args.label: ABLE enforcement activated on Q58 route

payload.event_name: ABLE enforcement activated on Q58 route
payload.description: Bus lane enforcement cameras activated on the Q58 route. Q58 was the second busiest route in 2022, carrying 6.7 million customers. With speeds at 7.6 MPH, it is 10% slower than the average Queens local and limited route.

tool_args.label: Off-peak service frequency increases on G, J, M lines

payload.description: First round of off-peak service frequency increases took effect on the G, J, and M lines earlier this month, reducing waiting times by up to 20% on weekends.
Two TransitTalk events in June 2023: one at Christopher St (1) in Greenwich Village on Pride, and another at 168th St (A, C, 1) on June 28 - the 16th event overall.
Expansion of the E-Hail Phase 2 program for paratransit, adding 2,400 customers. Pilot runs from August 1, 2023 to January 31, 2024.
Town hall event about zero-emission hydrogen-powered buses at Valentine Varian House, 3266 Bainbridge Avenue, in the Bronx, organized by the Buses Zero Emissions team.

tool_args.label: June 2023 paid average daily subway ridership
June 2023 subway ridership change vs June 2022
Average paid weekday subway ridership June 2023

payload.raw_value_text: declined by 6.2% in June to 3.608 million from 3.845 million

tool_args.label: June 2023 subway line satisfaction - B line 65%

warnings: metric_claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids on metric payloads refer to route entities and should become explicit relations when supported. Suggested relation: route has_metric metric_claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

payload.description: Bus lane enforcement cameras (ABLE system) activated on the Q58 route on July 14. To date, over 269,000 tickets issued across 19 routes covering all five boroughs. Current routes with ABLE have seen average bus speed improvements of 5% above the borough.

tool_args.label: Wide Aisle Gates at Sutphin Blvd and Atlantic Ave

payload.description: First two Wide Aisle Gates installed at Sutphin Blvd (E, J, Z) and Atlantic Ave (2, 3, 4, 5, B, D, N, Q, R) stations. Two thousand customers use them each day.

tool_args.label: Tactile warning strips at platform edges

payload.description: Installed tactile warning strips at 35 platform edges in the last year, with many more to come.
Replaced every single lock in the emergency gate system - over 1,600 of them - with highest-security upgrades to eliminate counterfeit key evasion.
NaviLens wayfinding pilot expanded to make it easier for blind or low-vision customers, as well as those with limited English proficiency, to find bus stops and get service information.

tool_args.label: Off-peak service frequency increases on G, J, M lines

payload.project_name: Off-peak service frequency increases on G, J, M lines
payload.description: First round of off-peak service frequency increases took effect on the G, J, and M lines, reducing waiting times by up to 20% on weekends.

Preparing to deploy two hydrogen fuel cell buses, the first-ever at the MTA and in all of New York State, and installing a hydrogen fueling station at the Gun Hill depot in the Bronx. Buses will run on approximately eight routes serving disadvantaged communities in northeast and central Bronx.

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

payload.description: Started on about 150 buses across seven routes, expanded in 2023 to an additional 50 routes across every borough, now includes more than 1,000 buses - nearly 20% of fleet.
Ongoing events where President Davey and team speak directly to customers at subway stations. First event at 3 Av-138 St (6) in June 2022. Visited 16 subway stations through June 2023.
Expansion of E-Hail service to an additional 2,400 customers. Pilot runs August 1, 2023 to January 31, 2024.

tool_args.label: Speed increase pilot on tracks adjacent to work zones

payload.project_name: Speed increase pilot on tracks adjacent to work zones
payload.description: Pilot program to safely increase speeds on tracks adjacent to work from 10 mph to 15 mph, started in July 2023.

payload.claim_text: The Blue-Ribbon Panel on MTA Fare and Toll Evasion recommended increasing fare enforcement on local bus routes, in addition to more actively communicating the importance of payment to the public and better supporting low-income New Yorkers who need help paying transit fares.
payload.subject: Blue-Ribbon Panel on MTA Fare and Toll Evasion

payload.claim_text: MTAPD continues to focus their efforts in the subway system at 4 major hubs: Penn Station, Grand Central Terminal, Atlantic Av - Barclays Ctr, and Sutphin Blvd - Archer Av.
payload.subject: MTA Police Department

payload.claim_text: MTA will build on the historic $5 billion commitment to accessibility in our current Capital Plan, which will make an additional 70 stations accessible, and the 17 new accessible stations that have recently opened.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Plan accessibility

tool_args.label: MTA hydrogen fuel cell bus grant

payload.description: Grant from the New York State Energy Research and Development Authority to pilot Hydrogen Fuel Buses.

tool_args.label: Richard Davey is President of NYCT
Demetrius Crichlow is SVP of Subways at NYCT
Off-peak frequency increase project serves G, J, M subway lines
